cases.passage: A small town has a beautiful lake that attracts tourists from nearby cities. The town council has decided to charge an entry fee of Rs. 50 per person to visit the lake. The council also provides boat rides, which cost Rs. 200 per hour. Many tourists are willing to pay for the boat rides, but some are hesitant due to the high cost. The council wants to know how to price the boat rides to maximize revenue. However, the council also needs to consider the limited number of boats available, which can accommodate only 20 people at a time. If the council decides to offer a discount of 10% to groups of 10 or more people, how will it affect the revenue from boat rides?

questions.q: What is the main objective of the town council in pricing the boat rides?
opts: (A) To maximize the number of tourists visiting the lake
(B) To maximize revenue from boat rides
(C) To minimize the cost of providing boat rides
(D) To increase the number of boats available
questions.explanation: The council wants to maximize revenue from boat rides.

questions.q: How will a 10% discount on boat rides for groups of 10 or more people affect the revenue from boat rides?
opts: (A) It will increase revenue
(B) It will decrease revenue
(C) It will have no effect on revenue
(D) It will depend on the number of groups taking the discount
questions.explanation: A discount of 10% will decrease the revenue from boat rides.

questions.q: What is the main constraint faced by the town council while pricing the boat rides?
opts: (A) Limited number of tourists willing to pay for boat rides
(B) Limited number of boats available
(C) Limited budget for marketing the lake
(D) Limited time to decide on pricing
questions.explanation: The council has only 20 boats available, which can accommodate only 40 people at a time.

questions.q: What is the opportunity cost of the town council deciding to offer a discount of 10% to groups of 10 or more people?
opts: (A) Loss of revenue from boat rides
(B) Increased cost of providing boat rides
(C) Decreased number of tourists visiting the lake
(D) Increased number of boats available
questions.explanation: The council will lose revenue from boat rides due to the discount.

questions.q: How can the town council use the concept of price elasticity of demand to make a decision about pricing the boat rides?
opts: (A) By analyzing the demand for boat rides at different price levels
(B) By calculating the revenue from boat rides at different price levels
(C) By comparing the revenue from boat rides with other attractions in the town
(D) By analyzing the supply of boats available
questions.explanation: The council can use price elasticity of demand to analyze how changes in price affect the demand for boat rides.

cases.passage: A local farmer, Mr. Sharma, grows wheat on his 100-acre farm. The market price of wheat is Rs. 200 per quintal. Mr. Sharma has two options: he can either sell his wheat at the current market price or store it for 6 months to sell it at a higher price of Rs. 250 per quintal. However, storing the wheat for 6 months will incur a cost of Rs. 10,000. What should Mr. Sharma do to maximize his profit?

questions.q: What is the main decision that Mr. Sharma needs to make regarding the wheat on his farm?
opts: (A) Whether to sell the wheat at the current market price or store it for 6 months
(B) Whether to grow wheat or another crop on his farm
(C) Whether to sell the wheat at a higher price or a lower price
(D) Whether to store the wheat for 6 months or not
questions.explanation: Mr. Sharma needs to decide whether to sell the wheat at the current market price or store it for 6 months to sell it at a higher price.

questions.q: What is the opportunity cost of Mr. Sharma deciding to store the wheat for 6 months?
opts: (A) Loss of revenue from selling wheat at the current market price
(B) Increased cost of storing wheat for 6 months
(C) Decreased profit from selling wheat at the higher price
(D) Increased number of customers willing to buy wheat
questions.explanation: The opportunity cost is the loss of revenue from selling wheat at the current market price.

questions.q: What is the main factor that will affect Mr. Sharma's decision about storing the wheat for 6 months?
opts: (A) The current market price of wheat
(B) The cost of storing wheat for 6 months
(C) The price at which Mr. Sharma can sell wheat after 6 months
(D) The quantity of wheat on Mr. Sharma's farm
questions.explanation: The price at which Mr. Sharma can sell wheat after 6 months will affect his decision.

questions.q: What is the concept that Mr. Sharma can use to make a decision about storing the wheat for 6 months?
opts: (A) Opportunity cost
(B) Price elasticity of demand
(C) Marginal analysis
(D) Consumer surplus
questions.explanation: Mr. Sharma can use marginal analysis to compare the additional revenue from selling wheat at the higher price with the additional cost of storing wheat for 6 months.

questions.q: What will happen to Mr. Sharma's profit if he decides to store the wheat for 6 months?
opts: (A) It will increase
(B) It will decrease
(C) It will remain the same
(D) It will depend on the cost of storing wheat for 6 months
questions.explanation: If Mr. Sharma decides to store the wheat for 6 months, his profit will decrease due to the additional cost of storing wheat.
